curation_notes: Pre-assigned as integer-overflow from CWE-681, but the patch fixes a missing negative exponent guard in the BigInteger modular exponentiation function (bnModPow in ext/jsbn2.js). The function ignores the sign of the exponent and processes only the magnitude bits, producing incorrect results that break signature verification. Reclassified to crypto-weakness because the root cause is a flawed implementation of a cryptographic primitive, not an integer overflow. Primary location derived from the patch diff. No sibling files contain the same vulnerability pattern; jsbn.js has bnModPowInt which takes plain integers, not BigIntegers.
